$comment: Example layout demonstrating context-sensitive segment aliasing (ADR-034). PROBLEM: in this feed an AD01 segment that appears BEFORE an EM01 is an ordinary postal address, but an AD01 that appears AFTER an EM01 is an email-related address. They share the same on-wire name (AD01) and the same byte layout, so the multiset comparator would lump them into one bucket — a postal-address difference could then mask an email-address difference, or vice versa. The on-wire bytes are fixed by the upstream system; we cannot rename AD01 at the source. SOLUTION: declare both AD01 and a same-sized EMAD segment, then add a segment_aliases rule. At parse time the engine scans each record's segments in order; once EM01 has appeared, every subsequent AD01 in that record is renamed to EMAD in memory (the bytes on disk are untouched). The trigger is per-record and once-armed-stays-armed: it resets at each record boundary, and a record that has AD01 but no EM01 keeps AD01. Drop this in as a layout_file_A.json / layout_file_B.json variant. See docs/decisions.md ADR-034 and the round-trip tests in tests/test_layout.py / tests/test_pipeline.py.

segments.name: AD01
segments.$comment: Address segment. An AD01 BEFORE any EM01 in the record is a postal address and is compared under the name AD01. An AD01 AFTER an EM01 is renamed to EMAD by the segment_aliases rule below. AD01 and EMAD MUST declare the same size (77) — the rename only changes the comparison bucket, not the bytes.

segments.name: EM01
segments.$comment: Email segment. Its presence is the trigger: once an EM01 has been seen in a record, subsequent AD01 segments in that same record are treated as EMAD.

segments.name: EMAD
segments.$comment: Logical-only address bucket for AD01-after-EM01. EMAD never appears on the wire — no record on disk contains the literal name EMAD. It exists in the layout solely so the engine has a declared segment (with a matching 77-byte size and identical field names) to rename post-EM01 AD01 instances into. Field names must match AD01's so the per-field comparison lines up.

segment_aliases.$comment: Rename rule: every AD01 that appears AFTER an EM01 in the same record becomes EMAD for comparison and reporting. wire_name and logical_name must both be declared in segments[] above and must share the same size; after_segment must also be declared. At most one alias is allowed per wire_name.
